    36→export const generateMetadata = async (props: {
    37→  params: Promise<PageParams>

    76→export default definePrerenderPage<PageParams>()({
    77→  fetcher(params) {

    81→  Component: ({ data, children }) => (
    82→    <TocHeadingStrategyProvider
    83→      contentFormat={data.contentFormat}
    84→      hasContent={!!data.content}
    85→    >
    86→      <PageColorGradient seed={data.title + data.subtitle} />
    87→      <CurrentPageDataProvider data={data} />
    88→      <div className="relative flex min-h-[120px] w-full">
    89→        <PageLoading>
    90→          <div className="relative w-full min-w-0">
    91→            <RoomProvider roomName={buildRoomName(data.id)}>
    92→              <WrappedElementProvider eoaDetect>
    93→                <article
    94→                  className={clsx(data.contentFormat !== 'lexical' && 'prose')}
    95→                >
    96→                  <header className="mb-8">
    97→                    <BottomToUpSoftScaleTransitionView
    98→                      lcpOptimization
    99→                      delay={0}
   100→                    >
   101→                      <PageTitle />
   102→                    </BottomToUpSoftScaleTransitionView>

 `./apps/web/src/app/[locale]/(page-detail)/[slug]/pageExtra.tsx`

```text
     1→'use client'
     2→
     3→import type { Image } from '@mx-space/api-client'
     4→import { useTranslations } from 'next-intl'
     5→import type { PropsWithChildren } from 'react'
     6→import { Fragment, useMemo } from 'react'
     7→
     8→import { useFocusReading } from '~/atoms/hooks/reading'
     9→import { GoToAdminEditingButton } from '~/components/modules/shared/GoToAdminEditingButton'
    10→import { WithArticleSelectionAction } from '~/components/modules/shared/WithArticleSelectionAction'
    11→import { Link } from '~/i18n/navigation'
    12→import { noopArr } from '~/lib/noop'
    13→import { MarkdownImageRecordProvider } from '~/providers/article/MarkdownImageRecordProvider'
    14→import { useCurrentPageDataSelector } from '~/providers/page/CurrentPageDataProvider'
    15→import { useAggregationSelector } from '~/providers/root/aggregation-data-provider'
    16→
    17→import FullPageLoading from '../loading'
    18→
    19→export const PageLoading: Component = ({ children }) => {
    20→  const id = useCurrentPageDataSelector((p) => p?.id)
    21→
    22→  if (!id) {
    23→    return <FullPageLoading />
    24→  }
    25→
    26→  return children
    27→}
    28→
    29→export const FocusReadingEffect = () => {
    30→  useFocusReading()
    31→  return null
    32→}
    33→
    34→export const MarkdownImageRecordProviderInternal = (
    35→  props: PropsWithChildren,
    36→) => {
    37→  const images = useCurrentPageDataSelector(
    38→    (data) => data?.images || (noopArr as Image[]),
    39→  )
    40→  if (!images) return null
    41→
    42→  return (
    43→    <MarkdownImageRecordProvider images={images}>
    44→      {props.children}
    45→    </MarkdownImageRecordProvider>
    46→  )
    47→}
    48→
    49→export const PageSubTitle = () => {
    50→  const subtitle = useCurrentPageDataSelector((data) => data?.subtitle)
    51→  return (
    52→    <p className="text-center text-lg text-neutral-600/70 dark:text-neutral-400 lg:text-left">
    53→      {subtitle}
    54→    </p>
    55→  )
    56→}
    57→export const PageTitle = () => {
    58→  const title = useCurrentPageDataSelector((data) => data?.title)
    59→  const id = useCurrentPageDataSelector((data) => data?.id)
    60→  return (
    61→    <>
    62→      <h1 className="text-balance text-center lg:text-left">{title}</h1>
    63→      <GoToAdminEditingButton
    64→        className="absolute -top-6 right-0"
    65→        id={id!}
    66→        type="pages"
    67→      />
    68→    </>
    69→  )
    70→}
    71→
    72→export const PagePaginator = () => {
    73→  const t = useTranslations('note')
    74→  const currentPageTitle = useCurrentPageDataSelector((d) => d?.title)
    75→  const pageMeta = useAggregationSelector((d) => d.pageMeta)
    76→  const pages = useMemo(() => pageMeta || [], [pageMeta])
    77→  const indexInPages = pages.findIndex((i) => i.title === currentPageTitle)
    78→  const n = pages.length
    79→  const hasNext = indexInPages + 1 < n
    80→  const hasPrev = indexInPages - 1 >= 0
    81→  return (
    82→    <div
    83→      data-hide-print
    84→      className="relative mt-8 grid h-20 select-none grid-cols-2"
    85→    >
    86→      <div className="justify-start">
    87→        {hasPrev && (
    88→          <Fragment>
    89→            <Link
    90→              className="flex flex-col justify-end text-left leading-loose"
    91→              href={`/${pages[indexInPages - 1].slug}`}
    92→            >
    93→              <span className="text-accent">{t('look_back')}</span>
    94→              <span>{pages[indexInPages - 1].title}</span>
    95→            </Link>
    96→          </Fragment>
    97→        )}
    98→      </div>
    99→      <div className="justify-end">
   100→        {hasNext && (
   101→          <Fragment>
   102→            <Link
   103→              className="flex flex-col justify-end text-right leading-loose"
   104→              href={`/${pages[indexInPages + 1].slug}`}
   105→            >
   106→              <span className="text-accent">{t('continue_reading')}</span>
   107→              <span>{pages[indexInPages + 1].title}</span>
   108→            </Link>
   109→          </Fragment>
   110→        )}
   111→      </div>
   112→    </div>
   113→  )
   114→}
   115→
   116→export const MarkdownSelection: Component = (props) => {
   117→  const id = useCurrentPageDataSelector((data) => data?.id)!
   118→  const title = useCurrentPageDataSelector((data) => data?.title)!
   119→  const canComment = useCurrentPageDataSelector((data) => data?.allowComment)!
   120→  const contentFormat = useCurrentPageDataSelector(
   121→    (data) => data?.contentFormat,
   122→  )
   123→  const content = useCurrentPageDataSelector((data) => data?.content)
   124→  return (
   125→    <WithArticleSelectionAction
   126→      canComment={canComment}
   127→      content={contentFormat === 'lexical' ? (content ?? undefined) : undefined}
   128→      contentFormat={contentFormat}
   129→      refId={id}
   130→      title={title}
   131→    >
   132→      {props.children}
   133→    </WithArticleSelectionAction>
   134→  )
   135→}
   136→
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-09 08:28:28Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I need to understand what styles the `prose` class applies to `h1` elements. The `prose` class is from Tailwind CSS Typography plugin. By default, it applies specific styling to headings including `h1`.

The default Tailwind prose h1 styles include:
- `font-size: 2.25em` (relative to the prose font-size)
- `margin-top: 0`
- `margin-bottom: 0.8888889em`
- `line-height: 1.1111111`
- `font-weight: 800`
- `color` (usually inherits or dark variant)


Since the prose base font-size is set to `1.1rem` in the custom CSS, the h1 would be `2.25 * 1.1rem = 2.475rem`. The user wants to move the `header` element out of the `article` tag, so the h1 will lose those prose styles and I'll need to apply equivalent styling directly to the `PageTitle` h1 element.

Looking at what Tailwind typography applies for h1 in prose—font-size of 2.25em, font-weight of 800, line-height of 1.1111111, and specific margin values—I can see the current `PageTitle` h1 only has `text-balance text-center lg:text-left`. I need to add the font sizing and weight to match what it would inherit from prose.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I understand the setup. The project uses `@tailwindcss/typography` plugin. The `prose` class gives h1 elements specific styling. I need to move the `header` out of the `article` tag and add appropriate styles to the h1 to match what prose would have given it.

The default Tailwind Typography prose h1 styles are:
- font-size: 2.25em
- font-weight: 800
- line-height: 1.1111111
- margin-top: 0
- margin-bottom: 0.8888889em

Since the prose base font-size is 1.1rem, the h1 would be approximately 2.25 * 1.1rem ≈ 2.475rem.


I'll move the header outside the article and apply matching Tailwind classes to the h1 element to replicate the prose styling. Closest Tailwind class would be... `text-4xl` is 2.25rem, but that's not exactly right. I could use an arbitrary value.
- `font-weight: 800` → `font-extrabold`
- `line-height: 1.1111111` → roughly `leading-tight` (1.25) or a custom value. Actually `leading-none` is 1, `leading-tight` is 1.25. The prose value 1.111 is between those. I could use `leading-[1.11]`.
- `margin-bottom: 0.8888889em` → about `mb-4` (1rem) or `mb-3.5` (0.875rem)

Actually wait - the h1 is inside a header that already has `mb-8`. Since the em units are relative to the heading's own font size, that margin translates to roughly 2rem in absolute terms. But the header's spacing already covers that, so I probably don't need to replicate the exact margin on the h1 itself. Let me just focus on getting the core visual properties right: font-size, font-weight, and line-height. and color.

For the color in prose dark mode, h1 gets `color: white` (prose-invert). In light mode, it's the default dark color.

The simplest approach: add the key prose h1 styles directly to the h1 element. Let me use:
- `text-[2.475rem]` or just keep it simpler with the visual match. Actually since the h1 won't be in a context with 1.1rem base font size anymore, I should use the computed value.

Wait, actually the `prose` class sets `font-size: 1.1rem` on the container, and then h1 inside prose is `2.25em` which is relative to the parent's font-size. So in the prose context, h1 is `2.25 * 1.1rem = 2.475rem`.

Outside prose, the h1 needs explicit sizing since it won't inherit that context. I'll move the header out of the article and apply Tailwind classes directly to match the prose styling—something like `text-4xl` or `text-[2.475rem]` for size, `font-extrabold` for weight, and `leading-[1.11]` for line-height to replicate what prose does by default. I'll use a straightforward approach: compute the final values by multiplying the prose base size with the h1 multiplier, then apply Tailwind classes like `text-[2.475rem]` for the font size, `font-extrabold` for weight, and `leading-[1.11]` for line height, while the color handling is already covered by the existing dark mode setup.

For the spacing, the h1's margin-bottom in prose creates the gap between the title and subtitle, which works out to around `mb-8` or `mb-9`, so I need to add that margin to the h1 element itself to maintain the proper spacing.

Now I'm thinking through the final approach: move the header outside the article element in layout.tsx, then add the prose-equivalent h1 styles to PageTitle. The subtitle already has explicit styling classes, so it won't need prose styles. I should also consider whether the header needs to stay within the WrappedElementProvider for the end-of-article detection to work properly.
